title: Cross-Salesforce Org Sharing Prevention
story: As a Salesforce administrator, I want the system to prevent users from different Salesforce organizations from sharing data, so that tenant data isolation is maintained and cross-org data leakage is prevented.
description:
Implement Salesforce organization ID validation during the invitation acceptance flow to prevent cross-org data sharing.

When a user accepts an invitation, the system validates that their Salesforce OrgID matches the OrgID of the inviting account. If the organizations don't match, the invitation is rejected with an explanatory error page and the account owner is notified via email.

The implementation also handles:
- Temporary user creation during invitation (no account created until Salesforce authentication)
- Expired/used invitation links showing an appropriate message
- Support for multiple invitations to the same email from different accounts
- Bidirectional data sharing (both users become account owners who can invite each other)
acceptance_criteria: System validates Salesforce OrgID match during invitation acceptance
Users from different Salesforce orgs are blocked from sharing data
Rejected users see an explanatory error page about org mismatch
Account owner receives email notification when an invitation is rejected
Used invitation links show an 'expired or already used' message
Users can receive and accept invitations from multiple accounts
Each user maintains their own account and can invite others
ux_changes: Error page displayed when a cross-org invitation is rejected, explaining that DataTools Pro does not allow co-mingling users from different Salesforce orgs. Expired invitation links show an appropriate message.
middleware_changes: Invitation acceptance flow now validates Salesforce OrgID match between inviter and invitee. Rejection triggers email notification to the account owner. Temporary user records are created during invitation for permission assignment.
implementation_notes: The Salesforce OrgID (not instance_url) is used for validation because instance URLs can technically change. A test user was created for cross-org testing. The system creates a temporary user record during invitation to assign permissions before the user authenticates.
comment_summary: Extensive discussion about the invitation flow, org validation, and temporary user creation. Developer implemented org matching, error pages, and rejection emails. Product clarified that users are 1:1 with Salesforce instances and same-org users share the same instance URL.
